text:
Market today: Chips are getting hit hard - Samsung and SK Hynix down 7%+ is the real story, and that rout is spreading from Wall Street. Your sector (semis/tech) is the epicenter today, not a bystander. Macro noise from Russia/Ukraine adds risk-off pressure but the chip selloff is the direct threat.

Your holdings: SOXL (-14.52%) is your biggest concern - it's a 3X leveraged ETF already deep in the red, stop at $200, and today's chip rout could accelerate the damage fast; margin makes this hurt triple. SPAL (-13.72%) is brand-new, very volatile leveraged ETF/IPO-adjacent, barely above its $21.38 stop - one bad tick and you're out. SMH (-2.5%) is your cleaner chip exposure but also bleeding today.

Watch: SOXL $200 stop - do not let it slide through. Also watch whether the Samsung/SK Hynix selloff bleeds further into US-listed semis by close.

Bottom line: Protect capital on your leveraged losers today - this is not the day to add chip exposure.
plays: SOXL: Your $200 stop is live and close - chip rout is active right now. Consider tightening or exiting partial position before it hits; margin amplifies every point down.
SPAL: $21.38 stop is right there. Fast-moving leveraged name in a risk-off tape - respect the stop, no adding here.
No new buys today - chip sector under active pressure; scanner setups like TSM and ARM exist but the 1H trend is already down on both, wrong entry timing.
